reasoning:
The base value for this question is the May 11, 2025 value, which was not known at the time the question was generated, but the latest published index (May 8) is 4.26%. The historical trend of the 90-day AA Commercial Paper has it slightly climbing since early March and the local momentum model predicts a ~0.68 to 0.70 probability of a minor increase above the 4.26% mark by May 18. I am using exactly 0.68 for May 18. 
For the June 10 deadline, the FOMC is held June 17-18, meaning there are no expected policy shifts between May 11 and June 10. The CP rate typically floats near the Fed Funds range (currently 4.25%-4.50%). Because the reference rate 4.26% is near the low end of this range, it's more likely to drift slightly up than to drift down further. Hence, June 10 has a >50% clear chance (0.59) of being higher than 4.26%.
For August 9 and November 7, the probability of the Fed cutting rates earlier in the year has decreased (currently ~28% for June, maybe 50% for July), but there remains a strong likelihood of at least 1-2 rate cuts (of 25 bps) by the end of the year if economic data holds steady. If the Fed cuts rates, the target range will fall to 4.00-4.25% or 3.75-4.00% making values above 4.26% far less likely. Thus, the risk of at least 1 rate cut pulls the probability down. August 9 gets 0.48 (almost equal chance of rate cut or held fast), while November gets 0.38 (leaning toward a rate cut by then). 
This fits the monotonic decrease in probability requested by the instructions.
